headline: Delta Air Lines Hikes Bag Fee as Industry Tries to Offset Surging Fuel Costs
summary: Delta Air Lines  is the latest carrier to raise its fees for checked luggage as the industry grapples with rising jet fuel prices brought on by the Iran war.  Delta said Tuesday it will increase checked luggage fees by $10 to $45 for a first item and $55 for a second item.  Delta didn’t immediately respond to request for comment from Barron’s.

headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Apple, UnitedHealth, Broadcom, Arm, Delta, Intel, Arista, and More
summary: Stocks were sliding Tuesday after President Donald Trump declared that “a whole civilization will die tonight” ahead of his 8 p.m. ET deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.  Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

Apple  tumbled 3.4% after Nikkei Asia reported that the iPhone maker’s long-awaited foldable phone had encountered engineering challenges and could face shipping delays.  The federal government on Monday pledged to pay insurers in the Medicare program an average increase of 2.48% next year, well above an originally proposed rate of just 0.09% in January.

summary: Delta Air Lines will lead off on Wednesday, providing a snapshot of how surging jet fuel prices have cut into profits.  “The Iran conflict has flipped the airline industry on its head,” Melius Research analyst Conor Cunningham said in a note on Monday.  Delta and United Airlines, the industry’s biggest profit generators, are charging more for checked bags.

content.title: Airlines cut flights, raise fees as jet fuel costs soar
content.summary: Jet fuel prices in the U.S. have nearly doubled since the U.S. and Israel attacked Iran in February, forcing carriers to trim schedules

headline: Delta Air hikes checked baggage fees as jet fuel prices soar
summary:
STORY: Delta on Tuesday said it will raise fees for checked bags on domestic and select international flights as the airline seeks to offset soaring fuel costs linked to the war in the Middle East.

It's the first hike in Delta's domestic baggage fees in two years and follows similar moves by rivals United Airlines and JetBlue.

Airlines worldwide are grappling with sharply higher fuel prices after tensions in the Middle East disrupted the delivery of oil through the Strait of Hormuz.

That's driven up airlines' operating costs and squeezed margins.

:: $85.00- $90.00

Jet fuel had averaged about $85 to $90 a barrel before the U.S. and Israel launched their first strikes on Iran in February.

:: $150.00- $200.00

But jet fuel has surged to around $150 to $200 a barrel in recent weeks.

Delta said fees for first and second checked bags on domestic and select short-haul international flights will increase by $10 each, while the cost of a third checked bag will rise by $50.

As a result, the fee for a first checked bag will increase to $45, the second to $55 and the third to $200.

The new prices go into effect on Wednesday.

content.title: Delta Air hikes checked baggage fees as jet fuel prices soar
content.summary:
STORY: Delta on Tuesday said it will raise fees for checked bags on domestic and select international flights as the airline seeks to offset soaring fuel costs linked to the war in the Middle East.

:: $150.00- $200.00

headline: Delta joins the growing list of US airlines raising checked bag fees as jet fuel costs soar
summary: Delta Air Lines announced Tuesday it is raising checked baggage fees, part of a broader wave of U.S. carriers responding to higher jet fuel prices tied to the war in the Middle East.  Beginning Wednesday, most passengers on domestic and short-haul international routes will pay $10 more for their first and second checked bags, and $50 more to check a third.  “These updates are part of Delta’s ongoing review of pricing across its business and reflect the impact of evolving global conditions and industry dynamics,” the carrier said in a statement.

content.title: Delta joins the growing list of US airlines raising checked bag fees as jet fuel costs soar
content.summary: Delta Air Lines announced Tuesday it is raising checked baggage fees, part of a broader wave of U.S. carriers responding to higher jet fuel prices tied to the war in the Middle East.  Beginning Wednesday, most passengers on domestic and short-haul international routes will pay $10 more for their first and second checked bags, and $50 more to check a third.  “These updates are part of Delta’s ongoing review of pricing across its business and reflect the impact of evolving global conditions and industry dynamics,” the carrier said in a statement.

headline: Delta Air Lines Hikes Bag Fees. Thank Higher Jet Fuel Costs.
summary: Delta Air Lines  is the latest carrier to raise its fees for checked luggage as the industry grapples with rising jet fuel prices brought on by the Iran war.  On Tuesday, Delta said baggage fees will increase by $10 each for the first two items, to $45 for the first item and to $55 for a second item.  “These updates are part of Delta’s ongoing review of pricing across its business and reflect the impact of evolving global conditions and industry dynamics,” a Delta spokesperson tells Barron’s.
